channel.title: Nature Physics
channel.description: Nature Physics offers news and reviews alongside top-quality research papers in a monthly publication, covering the entire spectrum of physics. Physics addresses the properties and interactions of matter and energy, and plays a key role in the development of a broad range of technologies. To reflect this, Nature Physics covers all areas of pure and applied physics research. The journal focuses on core physics disciplines, but is also open to a broad range of topics whose central theme falls within the bounds of physics.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [8] Amplitude fluctuations reshape the lattice chiral response in a ferroaxial electronic crystal
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03253-z

Helicity-resolved Raman spectroscopy reveals dynamical coupling between charge-density-wave amplitude fluctuations and symmetry-distinct phonons in a ferroaxial van der Waals crystal. This resonant dressing amplifies the material’s planar chiral lattice response through the underlying electronic order.
item.dc:title: [8] Amplitude fluctuations reshape the lattice chiral response in a ferroaxial electronic crystal
item.description:
Authors: Authors not available in source RSS

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: CDW amplitude fluctuations and chiral lattice response in an electronic crystal.

Tags: #CDW #electroniccrystal #chiral #vanderWaals

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03253-z Helicity-resolved Raman spectroscopy reveals dynamical coupling between charge-density-wave amplitude fluctuations and symmetry-distinct phonons in a ferroaxial van der Waals crystal. This resonant dressing amplifies the material’s planar chiral lattice response through the underlying electronic order.

item.title: [8] Resonant chiral dressing by amplitude fluctuations in a ferroaxial electronic crystal
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03241-3

Symmetry rules usually prevent interactions between distinct vibrational modes. Now it is shown that charge order fluctuations can mix modes, enhancing the chiral lattice response in a ferroaxial electronic crystal.
item.dc:title: [8] Resonant chiral dressing by amplitude fluctuations in a ferroaxial electronic crystal
item.dc:creator: Francesco Barantani, Xinyue Peng, Emil Viñas Boström, et al.
Xinyue Peng
Emil Viñas Boström

Angel Rubio
Edoardo Baldini
item.description:
Last authors: Angel Rubio, Edoardo Baldini

Authors: Francesco Barantani, Xinyue Peng, Emil Viñas Boström, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Charge order fluctuations and enhanced chiral lattice response in an electronic crystal.

Tags: #CDW #electroniccrystal #chiral

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03241-3 Symmetry rules usually prevent interactions between distinct vibrational modes. Now it is shown that charge order fluctuations can mix modes, enhancing the chiral lattice response in a ferroaxial electronic crystal.
